Regions
Home

Regional Specialties

Discover the unique flavors and traditions of France's diverse culinary landscape

Provence

Famous for herbs, olive oil, and fresh Mediterranean flavors. Home to bouillabaisse and ratatouille.

Signature Dishes

RatatouilleBouillabaisseSalade Niçoise

Specialties

Herbs de Provence, olive oil, rosé wine

Burgundy

Famous for wine-based cooking, mustard, and rich, hearty dishes.

Signature Dishes

Coq au VinBeef BourguignonEscargots

Specialties

Red wine, Dijon mustard, Charolais beef

Normandy

Known for apples, dairy products, and seafood from the coast.

Signature Dishes

CamembertApple TartMoules Marinières

Specialties

Cheese, Calvados, cream, apples

Alsace

Famous for German-influenced dishes like choucroute and tarte flambée.

Signature Dishes

ChoucrouteFlammekuecheKugelhopf

Specialties

Sauerkraut, Riesling, tarte flambée

Did You Know?

France is divided into 18 administrative regions, each with its own unique culinary identity. From the seafood-rich coasts of Brittany to the wine valleys of Bordeaux, every region offers a distinct taste of France.

“La gastronomie est l'art d'utiliser la nourriture pour créer le bonheur.”
